success_surface: Successful multi-sheet cross-reference lookup with conditional logic and data integrity preservation
effective_behaviors: Pre-execution structural exploration before task implementation
Using openpyxl instead of pandas for cell-level operations to preserve data integrity
Systematic breakdown of complex task into discrete executable steps
Conditional logic handling for edge cases (no match found, default values)
Column letter-to-index verification before accessing cells
Post-execution verification and confirmation of written values
Managing worksheet state (active sheet) before saving
Explicit print statements for debugging and validation
evidence: Agent first explored spreadsheet structure including column headers and positions before implementing solution
Used load_workbook from openpyxl rather than pandas.read_excel() to maintain data types and avoid formula conversion
Broke task into 5 numbered steps with clear logic flow in final solution code
Handled both success paths (match found) and failure paths (no match, use default F3)
Verified column P=16, L=12, F=6 through exploratory code before using hard-coded indices
Printed verification output showing Sheet B F4 value after write operation
Set wb.active = wb.sheetnames.index('A') before saving per instructions
Used print statements at key decision points during execution

memory_items.title: Structural Exploration Before Implementation
memory_items.lesson: Always explore workbook structure (sheet names, column layouts, data types) before writing manipulation logic
memory_items.generalization: Use diagnostic code to verify actual data layout matches assumed structure; this prevents index errors and ensures correct column references

memory_items.title: openpyxl for Cell-Level Operations
memory_items.lesson: For granular cell access and modification, prefer openpyxl over pandas to preserve original data types and avoid formula-to-value conversion
memory_items.generalization: Use pandas for bulk transformations, openpyxl for direct cell reads/writes, style preservation, and when formulas must remain intact

memory_items.title: Stepwise Task Decomposition
memory_items.lesson: Break complex cross-sheet reference tasks into sequential numbered steps with intermediate validation
memory_items.generalization: Implement one logical operation at a time, print status after each step, and ensure dependencies between steps are handled explicitly

memory_items.title: Defensive Edge Case Handling
memory_items.lesson: Always implement fallback logic when primary search conditions fail (no match found scenarios)
memory_items.generalization: Define default values or alternative paths before executing main logic; handle None, empty strings, and type mismatches gracefully

memory_items.title: Output Verification Protocol
memory_items.lesson: After writing values, always read back and print critical cells to confirm successful modification
memory_items.generalization: Include verification code that reads target cells immediately after writing; this catches silent failures before file is closed

edits.content:
## Advanced Patterns

### Multi-Sheet Cross-Reference Workflows
- **Exploration First:** Always verify actual sheet structure (headers, column positions, data types) before implementing lookup logic. Use diagnostic loops to print sample rows.
- **Stepwise Implementation:** Break complex cross-sheet operations into numbered steps with intermediate print statements for debugging.
- **Default Value Strategy:** When primary search fails (no match found), implement explicit fallback to known default cells.
- **Column Index Verification:** Confirm letter-to-number mappings (A=1, B=2, etc.) through exploration before hard-coding indices.

### Cell-Level Manipulation Best Practices
- **openpyxl Over pandas:** For individual cell reads/writes, use openpyxl's `cell(row, column).value` pattern rather than pandas DataFrames to preserve exact data types and avoid silent conversions.
- **Verification Protocol:** After every write operation, immediately read back and print the modified cell value to confirm success before closing the workbook.
- **Sheet State Management:** If instructions specify which sheet should be active on save, set `wb.active` using the sheet name index before calling `save()`.
- **Conditional Logic Flow:** Implement if/else branches for all success and failure paths in search-and-write operations.
